meaning: A good thing that seemed bad at first
example: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise because it led me to a better career.

meaning: To decide to do something difficult or unpleasant that one has been putting off

meaning: To stop working on something

meaning: Feeling ill or sick
example: I'm going to stay in bed today because I am feeling a bit under the weather.

meaning: Something that is very easy to do
example: The math test was a piece of cake; I finished it in ten minutes.

meaning: A way to wish someone good luck, especially before a performance
example: You will do great in the school play tonight. Break a leg!

meaning: To be very expensive
example: I would love to buy that new bicycle, but it costs an arm and a leg.

word: let the cat out of the bag

example: We were planning a surprise party, but my brother let the cat out of the bag.

meaning: Happening very rarely

meaning: Said when a person appears just after being mentioned
example: We were just talking about Mary, and speak of the devil, here she comes!

meaning: To agree with someone
example: My brother and I don't always see eye to eye on which movie to watch.

meaning: Something that will never happen
example: I will clean my room every single day when pigs fly!

example: I read my English book on the train to work, killing two birds with one stone.

meaning: An ideal situation where you can enjoy two different things at the same time

meaning: To do something badly or cheaply to save time or money

meaning: To make a bad situation even worse

meaning: To go to sleep

meaning: To miss an opportunity

meaning: What you do is more important than what you say

meaning: Looking in the wrong place or accusing the wrong person

meaning: To avoid talking about what is important

meaning: To destroy a relationship in a way that it cannot be fixed

example: I wish I could be a fly on the wall during their secret meeting.

meaning: To control your emotions and calm down
example: You are panicking over a small spider; you need to get a grip!

word: get a kick out of something
meaning: To enjoy or be amused by something
example: My grandfather gets a real kick out of watching me play football.

meaning: To escape punishment for doing something wrong
example: The youngest child is so cute that she gets away with murder.

meaning: To hear a secret or rumour
example: If mom gets wind of our broken lamp, we will be in big trouble.

meaning: To fail completely and spectacularly
example: Without any preparation, his presentation went down in flames.

meaning: Rushing causes mistakes
example: Take your time doing your homework; haste makes waste.

meaning: To force yourself not to say something
example: I wanted to argue with him, but I decided to hold my tongue.

meaning: In a difficult situation
example: I left my homework at home, so I am in a pickle now.

meaning: To join others in doing something that is currently popular
example: When that video game became popular, everyone jumped on the bandwagon and bought it.

word: keep your eye on the ball
meaning: To stay focused on what is important
example: To succeed in your exams, you need to keep your eye on the ball.

meaning: To accept that other people have their own ways of doing things
example: We have different opinions on the movie, but let's just live and let live.

meaning: Think carefully before making a big decision
example: You should check the depth of the pool before diving in; look before you leap.

meaning: Very nervous and anxious while waiting for something
example: I was on pins and needles waiting for the test results.

word: out of the frying pan and into the fire
meaning: To move from a bad situation to a worse one
example: By switching from a difficult class to an even harder one, she went out of the frying pan and into the fire.

word: put all your eggs in one basket
meaning: To risk everything on a single idea or plan
example: You should apply to many schools; don't put all your eggs in one basket.

meaning: To be bought very quickly and in large numbers
example: The new toys are selling like hotcakes at the store.

meaning: Doing something dangerous or risky
example: You are skating on thin ice by talking back to the teacher.

meaning: To be the most popular or successful part of an event
example: Her beautiful singing managed to steal the show at the concert.

meaning: To hear news from other people rather than directly
example: I heard through the grapevine that we might get a new teacher soon.

meaning: Done secretly and often illegally
example: He was paid under the table so he wouldn't have to pay taxes.

meaning: Very busy or deeply involved in something
example: I am up to my neck in homework tonight.

meaning: To refuse to take any more responsibility for something
example: He caused the problem, so I am going to wash my hands of the whole situation.
